variables_used.region_hint: USA + Canada, with Boston/New England emphasis
variables_used.sector_focus: transportation, aviation, healthcare, higher ed, water, federal

company.name: The Walsh Group
company.brief: Founded in 1898 by Matthew Myles Walsh as a carpentry business in Chicago, The Walsh Group is a fourth-generation family-owned general contracting enterprise. The company incorporated Walsh Construction in 1949 and has grown to become one of the largest contractors in North America, ranked No. 16 on the ENR Top 400 Contractors list in 2025 with $7.69 billion in 2024 construction revenue.

description.value: Founded in 1898 by Matthew Myles Walsh as a carpentry business in Chicago, The Walsh Group is a fourth-generation family-owned general contracting enterprise. The company incorporated Walsh Construction in 1949 and has grown to become one of the largest contractors in North America, ranked No. 16 on the ENR Top 400 Contractors list in 2025 with $7.69 billion in 2024 construction revenue.

divisions.name: Walsh Construction
divisions.brief: Walsh Construction, incorporated in 1949 in Chicago, is one of the largest and most respected general contracting, construction management, and design-build firms in North America. It operates out of regional offices across the United States, working with union labor and union subcontractors in the building, transportation, and water sectors.

focus.value: Transportation, Aviation, Transit/Rail, Healthcare, Higher Education, Water/Wastewater, Building/Commercial, Federal/Military

address.value: 929 West Adams Street, Chicago, Illinois 60607

divisions.name: Archer Western
divisions.brief: Archer Western, established in 1983, maintains a central corporate office in Atlanta, Georgia, and serves clients across seven regional offices. An open-shop contractor operating primarily in the southern and western United States, Archer Western is recognized as a national expert in building, transportation, and water sector projects.

focus.value: Transportation, Aviation, Water/Wastewater, Building/Commercial, Transit/Rail, Federal/Military

divisions.name: Walsh Canada
divisions.brief: Walsh Canada was established in 2009 in Toronto, Ontario, expanding The Walsh Group's reach beyond the United States. On-Site Magazine ranks Walsh as Canada's 24th largest contractor. The company has developed a track record of success in Canadian building, civil, transportation, and water sectors.

address.value: 310 North Queen Street, Suite 203S, Etobicoke, Ontario M9C 5K4

project_names: Women's College Hospital – Toronto, ON
Gilmore Lodge Long-Term Care Home – Ontario
ZEB Bus Charging Facility – Ontario

divisions.name: Walsh Federal LLC
divisions.brief: Walsh Federal LLC is The Walsh Group's dedicated federal contracting entity, holding multiple NAVFAC and USACE IDIQ multiple-award construction contracts. It delivers military construction and federal facility projects across the United States including hangars, barracks, and technical facilities.

projects.name: Nut Island Headworks Odor Control and HVAC Improvements
projects.brief: This $57.6 million (bid amount; approximately $61.6 million per ENR) project upgraded the Nut Island Headworks ventilation and odor control facilities for the MWRA. Key components included four 55,000 CFM fans, rehabilitation of three chemical wet scrubbers, replacement of five steel carbon absorbers with 10 fiberglass carbon-containing tanks, and installation of 15 custom air handling units. The project won ENR New England's Best Water/Environment Project of 2024.

projects.name: West Parish Water Treatment Plant
projects.brief: A $298 million (construction contract; full project ~$325 million) new 65 MGD dissolved air flotation (DAF) filtration water treatment plant replacing a 50-year-old facility. The project will serve roughly 250,000 water customers in Springfield and surrounding communities and is described as the most significant public infrastructure project in western Massachusetts in the last 25 years.

funding_source.value: Springfield Water and Sewer Commission / state/federal grants

projects.name: Washington Bridge Rebuild – I-195 Westbound
projects.brief: Walsh Construction and design partner WSP won the $339 million design-build contract to replace the westbound span of the Washington Bridge on I-195 in Providence. The bridge was suddenly shut down in December 2023, disrupting Southern New England travel. Physical construction began June 10, 2026, with completion targeted for November 2028.

projects.name: USCG Station Boston Upgrade (Sentinel-Class FRC Accommodation)
projects.brief: Walsh Construction upgraded USCG Station Boston to accommodate a new fleet of Sentinel-class Fast Response Cutters. This federal military project was mentioned in the 2024 news archive as a completed project enabling the station to service the USCG's updated vessel fleet.

projects.name: Red Line Extension (RLE) – Chicago, IL
projects.brief: The CTA awarded a $2.9 billion design-build contract to Walsh-VINCI Transit Community Partners in August 2024 to extend the Red Line 5.6 miles south from 95th Street to 130th Street, including four new stations. Walsh Construction holds a 60% share with VINCI Construction Grands Projets holding 40%. This is the largest civil construction contract ever awarded by the CTA.

projects.name: CTA Red & Purple Modernization (RPM) Phase 1 – Chicago, IL
projects.brief: The Walsh-Fluor joint venture was named the Contractor of the Year by the Chicago Transit Authority in December 2025 for work on the $2.1 billion Red and Purple Modernization Phase 1 project. In 2025, four new Red Line stations officially opened as part of the RPM project.

projects.name: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 – New York, NY
projects.brief: A $1.02 billion design-build contract awarded in June 2026 to a Skanska-Traylor-Walsh joint venture to build the 106th Street Station shell and connecting tunnels for the Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 extension. The project extends the Q line to 125th Street in East Harlem.

projects.name: Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Project – Cincinnati, OH / Covington, KY
projects.brief: Walsh Construction and Kokosing were awarded the $3.1 billion progressive design-build contract (total project approximately $4.4 billion) for the Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Project in July 2023. The project involves improvements to five miles of I-71/75 in Kentucky and one mile of I-75 in Ohio, improvements to the historic Brent Spence Bridge, and construction of a new companion bridge. Groundbreaking ceremony was held in 2026.

owner_client.value: Ohio DOT (ODOT) and Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC)

owner.name: Ohio DOT (ODOT) and Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC)

projects.name: I-69 Finish Line Contract 5 – Indiana
projects.brief: Walsh Construction completed I-69 Finish Line Contract 5 in Indiana, which was recognized as the No. 3 Road Project of 2025 by Roads & Bridges Magazine. This project is part of the ongoing I-69 completion effort through Indiana connecting Indianapolis to the southwest.

projects.name: I-69 Ohio River Crossing – Indiana / Kentucky
projects.brief: INDOT awarded the nearly $202 million contract to ORX Constructors, a joint venture of Walsh Construction and Traylor Bros., Inc., for the I-69 Ohio River Crossing. A groundbreaking ceremony was held in 2024.

projects.name: South Shore Line Double-Track Project – Gary to Michigan City, IN
projects.brief: Walsh Construction and Herzog joint-ventured to deliver the $650 million South Shore Line Double-Track Project, adding a second mainline track over 16.9 miles of the South Shore Line between Gary and Michigan City, Indiana. The project included new stations, bridges, and electrified infrastructure. Ribbon cutting was held in 2024 with Governor Eric Holcomb.

projects.name: I-290/I-88 Interchange Reconstruction at I-294 (Tollway) – Hillside, IL
projects.brief: Walsh Construction leads the reconfiguration and reconstruction of the I-290/I-88 interchange at I-294, a critical interchange serving more than 330,000 vehicles daily. The $327.5 million contract (I-21-4836) is part of the $4 billion Illinois Tollway Central Tri-State Tollway (I-294) reconstruction program. Scope includes reconstruction of 28 bridges and 19 ramps.

projects.name: I-294 Northbound Interchange Reconstruction (I-21-4835) – Hillside, IL
projects.brief: Walsh Construction was awarded $205.9 million contract I-21-4835 for roadway and bridge reconstruction on the Tri-State Tollway (I-294) northbound I-294/I-290/I-88 interchange between Mile Post 30.5 (Roosevelt Road) and Mile Post 32.4 (St. Charles Road).

projects.name: Amtrak Sawtooth Bridges Replacement Project – Kearny, NJ
projects.brief: Amtrak selected the Skanska-Walsh-Herzog joint venture to conduct preconstruction activities for the $2 billion Sawtooth Bridges Replacement Project in Kearny, NJ. The project will construct three new bridges along 1.9 miles of rail between Newark Penn Station and Secaucus Junction, replacing aging infrastructure on the Northeast Corridor.

projects.name: OSU Wexner Medical Center University Hospital – Columbus, OH
projects.brief: The Walsh-Turner joint venture completed the $1.5 billion Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center University Hospital in February 2026. The 26-story, 1.9 million sq ft tower with 820 beds is the largest single-facility construction project in OSU history and the largest healthcare project completed by Walsh.

projects.name: Ryan Field Reconstruction – Evanston, IL
projects.brief: Walsh Construction is delivering the new Ryan Field stadium for Northwestern University in Evanston, Illinois. Groundbreaking occurred in 2024, and the final steel beam was placed in 2025. This is a high-profile university athletics project replacing the original Ryan Field.

projects.name: Hyperion Advanced Water Purification Facility – Los Angeles, CA
projects.brief: Walsh Construction completed the Hyperion Advanced Water Purification Facility in Los Angeles, California, celebrating completion with a ribbon-cutting ceremony in early 2026. The award-winning project strengthens resilience, protects public health, and benefits the Bayview community.

owner_client.value: City of Los Angeles / Bureau of Sanitation

owner.name: City of Los Angeles / Bureau of Sanitation

projects.name: Pure Water Project Las Virgenes–Triunfo – Calabasas, CA
projects.brief: In December 2025, Walsh Construction was approved to finalize design and start construction of the Pure Water Project Las Virgenes–Triunfo, which will deliver a new Advanced Water Purification Facility. Groundbreaking ceremony was held in 2026.

owner_client.value: Las Virgenes Municipal Water District / Triunfo Water and Sanitation District

owner.name: Las Virgenes Municipal Water District / Triunfo Water and Sanitation District

projects.name: CSVT Central Susquehanna Valley Transportation – Snyder County, PA
projects.brief: Walsh Construction is leading the southern section of PennDOT's Central Susquehanna Valley Transportation (CSVT) Project in Snyder County, Pennsylvania.

projects.name: LaGuardia Airport Terminal B – Western Concourse – New York, NY
projects.brief: Walsh Construction completed LaGuardia Airport Terminal B, including the Western Concourse with seven new gates. The terminal achieved historic recognition as the first airport project in the world to achieve LEED certification for building design and construction and won the National Academy of Construction's inaugural Recognition of Special Achievement Award in 2022.

owner_client.value: LaGuardia Gateway Partners / Port Authority of NY & NJ

owner.name: LaGuardia Gateway Partners / Port Authority of NY & NJ

projects.name: South Shore Line Caltrain Electrification / Peninsula Corridor – CA
projects.brief: Walsh Construction participated in the $2.44 billion Caltrain Modernization Program (CalMod) electrification project on the San Francisco Peninsula, including overhead contact system installation. The project entered revenue service in fall 2024.

projects.name: Salesforce Tower Chicago – Chicago, IL
projects.brief: Walsh Construction completed the 1,422,000 sq ft Salesforce Tower Chicago, which includes 10,000 tons of structural steel and 5.3 million pounds of rebar. Time-lapse footage from 2017–2023 documented the construction from groundbreaking through topping out.

projects.name: Lawson House Historic Renovation – Chicago, IL
projects.brief: The $128 million renovation of the historic Lawson House by Walsh Construction and Farr Associates transformed 583 single-room occupancy units into over 400 modern affordable apartments featuring private kitchens and bathrooms. The project won awards for historic renovation and preservation efforts.

projects.name: John Carroll University Athletic, Wellness & Event Center – University Heights, OH
projects.brief: Walsh Construction unveiled the new Athletic, Wellness & Event Center at John Carroll University in 2025.

projects.name: Memorial Hospital Patient Tower – South Bend, IN
projects.brief: Walsh Construction is delivering a new patient tower at Memorial Hospital in South Bend, Indiana. The project achieved a topping-out milestone in 2025 when stakeholders celebrated the placement of the final structural steel.

projects.name: Four Seasons Private Residences Washington D.C.
projects.brief: Walsh Construction placed the final steel beam and topped out the Four Seasons Private Residences DC in 2025.

projects.name: NAVFAC Mid-Atlantic Contract N4008523C0050
projects.brief: Walsh Construction Group was awarded a $128 million contract by NAVFAC Mid-Atlantic in September 2023. The contract covers construction and related services with a 4-year duration and expected completion by October 2027.

projects.name: Ground Transportation Equipment Building – Warren, MI (USACE)
projects.brief: Walsh Construction Co. LLC was awarded a $59.2 million firm-fixed-price contract by USACE to construct a ground transportation equipment building in Warren, Michigan. Bids were solicited with four received.

projects.name: Walsh Federal LLC NAVFAC IDIQ MAC (N62473-21-D-1213)
projects.brief: Walsh Federal LLC holds NAVFAC IDIQ multiple-award construction contract N62473-21-D-1213 for military construction services. This award was confirmed in government contract records.

projects.name: VA Medical Center – Louisville, KY
projects.brief: Walsh-Turner joint venture began construction in 2021 on the new $840 million VA Medical Center in Louisville, Kentucky, replacing the Robley Rex VA Medical Center. The project was a major federal healthcare construction award.

projects.name: F-22A Maintenance Hangar at Langley AFB – Hampton, VA
projects.brief: Walsh Construction completed a six-bay hangar at Langley Air Force Base providing maintenance support for F-22A aircraft, announced in 2023.

projects.name: 44,000 sq ft Information Systems Facility at JBLM – Tacoma, WA
projects.brief: Walsh Construction completed the 44,000 sq ft Information Systems Facility supporting the Network Enterprise Center at Joint Base Lewis-McChord (JBLM) in Washington State.

projects.name: DFW Terminal F Expansion – Dallas-Fort Worth, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western is constructing DFW International Airport's first new terminal in 20 years using record-breaking modular construction. In August 2025, six prefabricated mega-modules were moved across the airfield and set in place. The largest module measured 278 feet by 136 feet and weighed 3,320 tons—a record-setting feat for terminal expansion at any airport worldwide. Archer Western was named DFW's Contractor of the Year for this project.

projects.name: DART Silver Line Regional Rail – Dallas-Fort Worth, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western Herzog (AWH) delivered the DART Silver Line, a 26-mile regional rail corridor connecting seven North Texas cities from Plano to DFW International Airport. The line officially opened October 25, 2025, transporting nearly 12,000 riders on its first day of operation.

projects.name: Big Creek Water Reclamation Facility Expansion – Roswell, GA
projects.brief: Archer Western and Brown and Caldwell in a design-build joint venture completed the $300 million Big Creek Water Reclamation Facility expansion, the largest single capital expansion project in Fulton County's history. The project expanded plant capacity from 24 MGD to 32 MGD. Ribbon cutting was held in 2024 and the project won multiple national awards.

owner_client.value: Fulton County Department of Public Works

owner.name: Fulton County Department of Public Works

projects.name: Howard Frankland Bridge Southbound Structure – Tampa, FL
projects.brief: Archer Western completed the new southbound Howard Frankland Bridge structure in Tampa, Florida. The 4,800-foot-long southbound span is designed to last 100 years and increases capacity to five lanes. The bridge opened to commuters in 2025.

projects.name: I-395 Signature Bridge – Miami, FL
projects.brief: Archer Western is constructing the landmark I-395 Signature Bridge in Miami, featuring distinctive arches rising along I-395. The joint venture completed the first of six arches in 2024. Drone footage captured arch construction progress in 2024.

projects.name: I-26 Connector – Asheville, NC
projects.brief: Archer Western is constructing the I-26 Connector in Asheville, North Carolina, which will transform vehicle, bicycle, and pedestrian access to and through downtown Asheville.

projects.name: Carolina Crossroads I-26 Reconstruction – South Carolina
projects.brief: Archer Western and United Infrastructure Group are constructing the first two phases of SCDOT's landmark Carolina Crossroads project, a major redesign of the I-26/I-20/I-126 interchange in South Carolina. The joint venture received top safety honors for the project.

projects.name: MARTA Rapid Bus Transit Project – Atlanta, GA
projects.brief: Archer Western joined MARTA and the City of Atlanta to break ground on the MARTA Rapid bus transit project in 2023, expanding Atlanta's rapid transit network.

owner_client.value: Metropolitan Atlanta Rapid Transit Authority (MARTA) / City of Atlanta

owner.name: Metropolitan Atlanta Rapid Transit Authority (MARTA) / City of Atlanta

projects.name: Sarasota-Bradenton Airport Baggage Handling System – Sarasota, FL
projects.brief: Archer Western reached substantial completion at the Baggage Handling System Consolidation Project at Sarasota-Bradenton International Airport in early 2025, incorporating the latest baggage screening technology.

projects.name: Cleburne WWTP Expansion – Cleburne, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western completed the Cleburne Wastewater Treatment Plant expansion and received two prestigious awards for the work in 2025.

projects.name: Lake Texoma Raw Water Pump Station – Denison, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western completed the $50 million Lake Texoma Raw Water Pump Station for the City of Denison, the largest capital project in the city's history. The project included a new intake pump station with vertical turbine pumps in Lake Texoma, 6,750 LF of 42-inch raw water pipeline, and associated infrastructure. Construction started in 2023 and was finished on time in 2026.

projects.name: South Cross Bayou WWTP Rehabilitation – St. Petersburg, FL
projects.brief: Archer Western completed the rehabilitation of 11 de-nitrification filters at the South Cross Bayou Wastewater Treatment Plant in St. Petersburg, Florida, including removal of old media, repair of internal systems, and installation of approximately 2,300 bags of new filter media.

projects.name: Santa Clara Creek Water Reclamation Facility Expansion – Seguin, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western completed the expansion of the Santa Clara Creek Water Reclamation Facility in Seguin, Texas, increasing capacity from 0.25 MGD to 0.75 MGD to support growth in northeast San Antonio.

projects.name: Lift Station 87 Wet Weather Flow Transfer – St. Petersburg, FL
projects.brief: Archer Western designed, permitted, and built the $11.5 million Lift Station 87 Wet Weather Flow Transfer facility in St. Petersburg, Florida, bolstering the city's sewer and wastewater capabilities in the Childs Park area.

projects.name: Hutto South Wastewater Treatment Plant – Hutto, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western and the City of Hutto celebrated the South Wastewater Treatment Plant groundbreaking in 2024, delivering a new 3 MGD facility to service residences and businesses in San Tan Valley, Arizona.

projects.name: Agua Nueva Water Reclamation Facility – Pima County, AZ
projects.brief: Archer Western serves as the strategic delivery partner to Jacobs Engineering for the Agua Nueva Water Reclamation Facility, a new 32 MGD facility at the Pima County Regional Wastewater Reclamation Campus in Tucson, Arizona.

owner_client.value: Pima County Regional Wastewater Reclamation Department (PCRWRD)

owner.name: Pima County Regional Wastewater Reclamation Department (PCRWRD)

projects.name: Panorama J Apartments – Flower Mound, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western celebrated the formal groundbreaking of the Panorama J Apartments in Flower Mound, Texas in 2025. Archer Western placed the final beam atop the 16-story tower.

projects.name: Simsboro Aquifer Water Treatment Plant – Bastrop, TX
projects.brief: Archer Western is constructing the new Simsboro Aquifer Water Treatment Plant in Bastrop, Texas, to provide a reliable and sustainable source of water for the city and surrounding areas. The project will specifically remove PFAS from source water.

projects.name: Women's College Hospital – Toronto, ON
projects.brief: Walsh Canada completed the Women's College Hospital project in Toronto, Ontario, one of the notable Canadian healthcare construction projects referenced by Walsh Group co-chairman Dan Walsh.

projects.name: ZEB Bus Charging Facility – Ontario, Canada
projects.brief: Walsh Canada completed a progressive design-build project to support charging for 120 zero-emission buses (ZEBs) in Ontario, Canada.

projects.name: University of Kentucky Markey Cancer Center – Lexington, KY
projects.brief: Walsh Construction is delivering the 550,000 sq ft new home for the University of Kentucky Markey Cancer Center, a major higher education and healthcare project. The project also includes a campus energy utility plant component. Walsh partners with University of Kentucky and Goodwill Kentucky for workforce development.

projects.name: San José–Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility – San Jose, CA
projects.brief: Walsh Construction is supporting Jacobs Engineering on the design-build modernization of the San José–Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. Construction progress photos were shared in 2023 and the project advanced in 2026.

owner_client.value: City of San José / City of Santa Clara

owner.name: City of San José / City of Santa Clara

memberships.brief: Associated General Contractors of America (AGC): Multiple AGC awards referenced in news releases
Design-Build Institute of America (DBIA): Multiple DBIA awards and Doug VanSlambrook certified DBIA Designated Design-Build Professional
Associated Subcontractors of America (ASA) Chicago: Walsh Construction's J.R. Snyder recognized with ASA Chicago Outstanding GC Project Manager Award in 2023
Underground Construction Association (UCA): Walsh Construction superintendent David Dorfman received 2025 Muddy Boots Award from UCA
AGC Culture of CARE: Walsh took the pledge to join AGC's Culture of CARE program in 2020
American Society for Surface Treatment (ASFONA): Walsh Group listed in ASFONA Associate Membership Directory

existing_software.brief: Procore: Construction management platform. Evidence: Walsh Group and Walsh Norwood office both listed on Procore's contractor network.

co_chairmen.name: Daniel J. Walsh
co_chairmen.title: Co-Chairman of the Board
co_chairmen.brief: Daniel J. Walsh is the co-chairman of The Walsh Group representing the third generation of family ownership, with more than 50 years of leadership within the company. He has been directly responsible for growing company revenues beyond $5 billion and securing major projects such as LaGuardia Airport Redevelopment.

co_chairmen.name: Matthew M. Walsh
co_chairmen.title: Co-Chairman of the Board
co_chairmen.brief: Matthew M. Walsh is the co-chairman and also CEO of The Walsh Group, representing the third generation of family ownership with more than 50 years of leadership. He co-founded the Walsh Foundation in 1990 to encourage minority and female students to enter construction and engineering.

other_executives.name: Tim Gerken
other_executives.title: CFO
other_executives.brief: Tim Gerken serves as CFO/CBO of The Walsh Group providing executive leadership, strategic planning, and oversight of corporate administrative and financial functions.

safety.culture_statement: Safety is our culture — The most important thing we can do every day is to send men and women home in the same shape as when they arrived. — Dan Walsh, Co-Chairman

awards.award: BX Safety Achievement Award – Ohio and Ohio River Valley (Walsh Construction)
Construction Safety Excellence Award and Safety on a High Hazard Project Award (Walsh Construction)
AGC Safety Leadership Award – Hays (Georgia, Archer Western affiliate)
Archer-United JV Top Safety Honors – Carolina Crossroads

safety.safety_week: Walsh Group is a founding member of Construction Safety Week (national construction industry event)

regional_office.address: 100 River Ridge Drive, Third Floor, Norwood, MA 02062

confirmed_projects: Nut Island Headworks Odor Control and HVAC Improvements – Quincy, MA (MWRA, $61.6M, completed 2024)
West Parish Water Treatment Plant – Westfield, MA (Springfield Water & Sewer Commission, $298M, started 2024)
Washington Bridge Rebuild – Providence, RI ($339M, design-build, awarded 2025, construction started June 2026)
USCG Station Boston Upgrade – Boston, MA (federal, completed 2024)
near_region_projects: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 – New York, NY ($1.02B, Skanska-Traylor-Walsh JV, 2026)
Amtrak Sawtooth Bridges – Kearny, NJ ($2B preconstruction, Skanska-Walsh-Herzog, 2025)
boston_new_england_summary.northeast_contact: Twidwell (VP/senior executive) oversees transportation projects in NY, NJ, CT, MA and eastern PA per 2023 news
boston_new_england_summary.notes: Walsh Construction maintains an active Boston/New England regional office in Norwood, MA. The office address is 100 River Ridge Drive, Third Floor, Norwood, MA 02062. Key confirmed Massachusetts projects include the MWRA Nut Island Headworks and West Parish Water Treatment Plant. The Providence Washington Bridge is a major recent award. DBE outreach contact at Norwood office is Carrie Munns (781-793-9988).

confirmed.evidence: Walsh Group and Walsh Norwood office both listed on Procore's contractor network

known_use_unconfirmed_source: Bluebeam (industry standard for Walsh-scale GC, not directly sourced)
Autodesk BIM 360 / ACC (referenced in industry context but not directly confirmed for Walsh)

technology_awards.award: Award for use of technology to strengthen teams, operations and people-first culture (2026)

technology_and_tools_full.vdc_capability: Walsh Group employs a Senior Virtual Design and Construction Manager; recognized for new technology implementation in 2020

firmographics_raw.brief: Founded in 1898 by Matthew Myles Walsh as a carpentry business in Chicago, The Walsh Group is a fourth-generation family-owned general contracting enterprise. The company incorporated Walsh Construction in 1949 and has grown to become one of the largest contractors in North America, ranked No. 16 on the ENR Top 400 Contractors list in 2025 with $7.69 billion in 2024 construction revenue.

special_rankings_2024: No. 1 Water Treatment and Desalination Plant Builder (ENR)

firmographics_raw.crain_chicago_ranking: No. 1 Construction Company in Chicago (Crain's Chicago Business 2025)
